lint.command: No lint script is defined in package.json or .factory/services.yaml for this repo.

blockingIssues.description: When `/coins/markets` serves seeded-bootstrap fallback rows, only requested `price_change_percentage_*_in_currency` fields are nulled. The default `price_change_percentage_24h_in_currency` field is still produced by the surrounding row builder via the standard row contract, so degraded rows can continue exposing a market-change value even though the feature's chosen policy nulls degraded market-value fields. The new regression in `tests/stale-data.test.ts` only checks `price_change_percentage_24h`, not the always-present `price_change_percentage_24h_in_currency`, so this compatibility gap is untested and can leak fresh-looking degraded data.

`runStartupPrewarm()` collapses every non-2xx inject result into `status: 'timeout'` via the inline `.then(...)` mapper, and the loop then breaks on that status. A real handler error or contract failure on the first target is therefore reported as a budget timeout and prevents later declared targets from running at all, so the implementation does not reliably prewarm the declared target set or expose accurate diagnostics/metrics evidence for validators.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update runtime-facing worker guidance to explicitly inspect startup prewarm surfaces (`src/services/startup-prewarm.ts`, diagnostics `startup_prewarm`, and `opengecko_startup_prewarm_*` metrics) when implementing or validating startup-latency features.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature startup-prewarm-hot-endpoints review noted that workers had to discover startup prewarm targets, diagnostics, and metrics ad hoc because shared guidance did not mention them. Evidence: Feature metadata for `startup-prewarm-hot-endpoints` assigns a runtime/api skill workflow; the transcript skeleton shows the worker loaded a runtime/api worker skill and then had to discover `src/services/startup-prewarm.ts`, `src/services/metrics.ts`, and diagnostics routes ad hoc. No `.factory/library/` note mentions prewarm surfaces or target attribution.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a mission note that startup-prewarm evidence lives in diagnostics `startup_prewarm` fields and `opengecko_startup_prewarm_*` metrics so validators know where to look.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature startup-prewarm-hot-endpoints review found the repo now exposes validator-facing prewarm evidence but mission guidance does not mention those surfaces. Evidence: Feature metadata for `startup-prewarm-hot-endpoints` assigns a runtime/api skill workflow; the transcript skeleton shows the worker loaded a runtime/api worker skill and then had to discover `src/services/startup-prewarm.ts`, `src/services/metrics.ts`, and diagnostics routes ad hoc. No `.factory/library/` note mentions prewarm surfaces or target attribution.

rejectedObservations.observation: The mission/library guidance says fallback work should extend `src/modules/market-freshness.ts`, but this feature encoded degraded-bootstrap classification partly in `src/services/runtime-diagnostics.ts` using runtime failure reason plus snapshot ownership. That convention is not documented clearly enough for future workers deciding where fallback/source-class policy should live.
